questions.question: Why does India experience seasonal reversal of winds?
questions.answer: Because of pressure differences between land and sea during different seasons.

questions.question: Why are winters milder in India compared to Central Asia?
questions.answer: Because the Himalayas block cold winds from Central Asia.

questions.question: Why do coastal areas experience less temperature variation?
questions.answer: Due to the moderating influence of the sea.

questions.question: Why does the Thar Desert have high day–night temperature variation?
questions.answer: Because it has a dry climate and sandy surface.

questions.question: Why do the Western Ghats receive heavy rainfall?
questions.answer: Because they lie in the path of the monsoon winds.

questions.question: Why does rainfall decrease from east to west in the northern plains?
questions.answer: Because monsoon winds lose moisture as they move westward.

questions.question: Why is Ladakh a cold desert?
questions.answer: Because it lies in the rain shadow area of the Himalayas.

questions.question: Why do houses in Rajasthan have thick walls?
questions.answer: To protect people from extreme heat.

questions.question: Why do houses in Assam have sloping roofs?
questions.answer: To allow rainwater to flow easily.

questions.question: Why are houses in Assam built on stilts?
questions.answer: To protect them from floods.

questions.question: Why do cyclones occur in the Bay of Bengal region?
questions.answer: Because warm ocean waters create low-pressure systems.

questions.question: Why is October heat experienced in India?
questions.answer: Because temperatures remain high after the monsoon withdraws.

questions.question: Why are floods common in some regions of India?
questions.answer: Due to heavy monsoon rainfall.

questions.question: Why is agriculture dependent on monsoon in India?
questions.answer: Because most crops depend on rainfall for water supply.

questions.question: Why do interior regions experience extreme climate?
questions.answer: Because they are far from the sea.

questions.question: Why does Meghalaya receive heavy rainfall?
questions.answer: Because moist monsoon winds strike the Khasi Hills.

questions.question: Why do farmers eagerly await the monsoon?
questions.answer: Because crops depend on monsoon rainfall.

questions.question: Why is the monsoon called a unifying bond?
questions.answer: Because it affects the entire country’s agriculture and lifestyle.

questions.question: Why do mountain regions receive snowfall?
questions.answer: Because temperatures are below freezing at high altitudes.

questions.question: Why do droughts occur in some regions of India?
questions.answer: Because they receive very little rainfall.

questions.question: Why do western disturbances bring winter rain?
questions.answer: Because they are cyclonic storms carrying moisture.

questions.question: Why is rainfall unevenly distributed in India?
questions.answer: Due to relief, wind direction and distance from the sea.

questions.question: Why does the Deccan Plateau receive less rainfall?
questions.answer: Because it lies in the rain shadow region.

questions.question: Why does India experience four distinct seasons?
questions.answer: Because of the monsoon wind system.

questions.question: Why is rainfall variability high in Rajasthan?
questions.answer: Because the region receives very low rainfall.
